_rationale: Iter-13 (DART-zdP14GNGfeo3): verify the compact `f`/`s` ref-key contract for context.save is honoured by both binaries. Direct probe in iter-13 confirmed Go REJECTS verbose `{file, symbol}` ref keys (`internal/types/context_manifest_types.go` ContextRef struct has `json:"f"` / `json:"s,omitempty"` — there is no `file`/`symbol` alias). C++ `manifest_from_json` (src/mcp/handlers_context.cpp:63-68) also reads only `f`/`s`. The compact-key contract is therefore mutual — this descriptor locks it. Note: the *output* manifest body still diverges between binaries (Go top-level keys `t/c/v/p/r/s` vs C++ `task/version/project_root/refs`; Go LineRange `{s,e}` vs C++ `{start,end}`; Go ref note `n` vs C++ `note`). Format divergence is out of scope for this iteration and tracked in the follow-up subtask filed against zdP14GNGfeo3 (manifest body shape parity). For this descriptor the diverging output keys are tier=ignore with rationale below; the parity guard is the envelope shape and the absence-of-error on the compact-key save.

tiers._rationale_stable: Envelope frame: content[].type='text' and isError must match. A successful save on both sides means both accepted the compact `{f, s}` ref shape — the contract under test. result.isError absent or false on both → both binaries accepted; both true → both rejected (still parity, surface for triage). The textual manifest body itself is ignore-tier this iter, see _rationale_ignore.
tiers._rationale_ignore: The manifest body returned by `to_string:true` differs between binaries in iter-13: Go emits compact top-level keys (t/c/v/p/r/s) per `internal/types/context_manifest_types.go` json tags; C++ emits verbose keys (task/version/project_root/refs) per `src/mcp/handlers_context.cpp` manifest_to_json. The save handler's success/failure envelope is parity-stable (both accept the compact ref keys, both error on verbose ref keys — probe-confirmed iter-13), but the body shape is a separate divergence filed as a follow-up subtask under zdP14GNGfeo3. When that follow-up lands, lift content[].text from ignore to stable here and replace this _rationale_ignore with the parity-fix rationale per iter-8 pattern (mcp/index_stats/basic.parity.json). Karpathy rule 6 (no silent fallback) — do not paper over: surface the gap in a descriptor + a tracked task, not in a hidden normaliser.
